body.title: Port aider auto-lint/test verdict gate into the reflection loop (P6)
body.description:
**Problem:** the editor self-reports done; nothing structural verifies the edit even parses — the silent-wrongness machine the thesis warns about. This is the VERDICT side of the nexus triple.

**Proposed shape:** wire an auto-lint (pyflakes/ruff shell-out per edited file) and the plan-named test into the reflection loop after apply (port the `auto_lint`/`auto_test` -> `reflected_message` wiring, base_coder.py:1599-1623). Lint errors re-enter reflection; lint-clean + named-test-green is the `verdict` column at the `test_green` rung of D-proof-program-grounding-spine's verdict_strength gradient.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/architect_editor.py (post-apply gate); new lint shell-out helper.
**Design rules:** the gate is deterministic, world-facing, moodless (warden material — compiles). The FIX loop it triggers stays LLM. ⛔ NO SQLITE.
**Scope boundary:** the lint/test verdict gate + reflection wiring. The nexus write of the verdict column is T-nexus-write-path-ds-architect-loop.
**Test plan:** red->green — an edit that lint-fails re-enters reflection with the lint error; a lint-clean + named-test-green edit yields a verdict record; the verdict is at the test_green rung.
**Completion criteria:** post-apply lint/test gate produces a deterministic verdict; lint failures re-enter reflection; verdict available to the nexus plan-row write.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Post-apply lint/test verdict gate (P6): new verdict_gate.py — after a clean apply, lint/compile (ruff/pyflakes shell-out if present, else py_compile parse check) + the plan-named pytest node; failures re-enter the SAME bounded reflection loop (cost <= cap+1 across all failure sources). Verdict rung rides the gradient unverified<compile_ok<lint_clean<test_green (failure: compile_error/lint_error/test_red; test_unknown caps at lint, never green, never reflects). The verdict is DATA on the DONE envelope + io_corpus (the nexus verdict column) — it does NOT flip DONE/escalate; the walk contract is unchanged. Proven red->green: a syntactically-broken applied edit earns compile_error (a failure rung), never a silent passing DONE — the anti-silent-wrongness invariant; test_green kept falsifiable by a red companion; test-couldn't-run distinguished from test-red.
